message:
feat(demo): integrate demo mode into sessions list page

When lab mode is disabled:
- Show DemoSessionsView with demo sessions instead of locked state
- Add "(Demo)" indicator in page header
- Update description to explain demo mode
- Skip Agent Mail fetching (not needed for demo)

When lab mode is enabled but auth fails:
- Continue showing LockedState with auth error reason

Implements brenner_bot-qtrk

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-02l","title":"TanStack Query: Create Session Kickoff Mutation","description":"# Goal
Create a useMutation hook for the session creation form submission, replacing the current server action pattern.

## Background
Currently, session creation uses a Next.js server action:
```typescript
// apps/web/src/app/sessions/new/page.tsx
async function sendKickoff(formData: FormData): Promise<void> {
  'use server';
  // ... validation and Agent Mail call
}
```

This works but lacks:
1. Loading state management
2. Error handling UI
3. Optimistic updates
4. Retry logic

## Implementation

### 1. Create useSessionMutation hook
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/mutations/useSessionMutation.ts
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';

interface SessionInput {
  threadId: string;
  hypothesis: string;
  priors: string;
  agent: string;
}

export function useSessionMutation() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient();
  
  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (input: SessionInput) => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/sessions', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify(input),
      });
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to create session');
      return response.json();
    },
    onSuccess: () => {
      // Invalidate session list if we have one
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['sessions'] });
    },
    onError: (error) => {
      // Could integrate with toast notifications
      console.error('Session creation failed:', error);
    },
  });
}
```

### 2. Create API route (if not using server action)
If we move away from server actions, create:
```typescript
// apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts
export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const body = await request.json();
  // ... validation and Agent Mail call
}
```

### 3. Update form component
```typescript
const mutation = useSessionMutation();

const handleSubmit = (data: SessionInput) => {
  mutation.mutate(data, {
    onSuccess: () => {
      // Show success, redirect, etc.
    },
  });
};

// In JSX:
{mutation.isPending && <LoadingSpinner />}
{mutation.isError && <ErrorMessage error={mutation.error} />}
```

## Considerations

### Server Actions vs API Routes
Decision: Keep server action for now, wrap with mutation for state.
TanStack Query can work with server actions via a wrapper.

### Optimistic Updates
For now, just show loading/success. Session list doesn't exist yet.
Future: Add optimistic session to list before confirmation.

### Error Types
Define specific error types:
- ValidationError (user input issues)
- NetworkError (Agent Mail unreachable)
- AuthError (not authenticated)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] useSessionMutation hook created
- [ ] Form shows loading state during submission
- [ ] Form shows error state on failure
- [ ] Success triggers appropriate UI feedback
- [ ] TypeScript types are complete

## Files to Create/Modify
- apps/web/src/hooks/mutations/useSessionMutation.ts (create)
- apps/web/src/app/sessions/new/page.tsx (use hook)
- apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts (optional)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:41.085061423-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:26:38.959003392-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:26:38.959003392-05:00","close_reason":"Infrastructure complete: Created useSessionMutation hook (hooks/mutations/), API route (/api/sessions/route.ts), and SessionForm client component. Page integration was attempted but reverted by pre-commit hook - page can be updated to use SessionForm component when hook issue is resolved. Pre-existing build error in DistillationViewer.tsx (unrelated) prevents full build validation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-02l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:18.672791522-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-02wy","title":"Bug: Jargon tests cover canonical component + stabilize unknown-term rendering","description":"## Problem

- The web app uses `apps/web/src/components/jargon.tsx` for inline glossary tooltips.
- The existing unit test suite in `apps/web/src/components/ui/jargon.test.tsx` was importing a different implementation (under `components/ui/`), so the real component behavior was effectively untested.
- The canonical component also had two reliability footguns:
  - Unknown terms returned a fragment (no wrapper element), making DOM assertions brittle and easier to collide with other test content.
  - `isMobile` defaulted to `false` until an effect ran, which can transiently route clicks to the desktop tooltip path on mobile-sized viewports (and can be flaky in tests).

## Fix

- Update the canonical Jargon component so unknown-term fallbacks render a stable `<span>` wrapper.
- Initialize `isMobile` from `matchMedia` in the `useState` initializer (still updated on resize via effect).
- Point `apps/web/src/components/ui/jargon.test.tsx` at the canonical component (`@/components/jargon`) and adjust expectations to match current tooltip/sheet UI.
- Use `within(container)` for the unknown-term test to avoid accidental cross-test DOM matches.","acceptance_criteria":"- `bun run test` passes in `apps/web`
- Unknown-term render produces a stable `<span>` wrapper
- Desktop focus shows tooltip content with glossary link
- Mobile tap opens sheet reliably","notes":"This intentionally does not delete the unused UI implementation file(s); deletion requires explicit user approval per AGENTS.md. Future cleanup can either (a) re-export the canonical component from `components/ui/jargon.tsx` or (b) delete the unused file(s) after explicit approval.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:04:21.136346573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T11:04:51.638027563-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T11:04:51.638027563-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: canonical Jargon hardened + tests now cover it"}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0b5","title":"Feature: Unit Tests for Web App Libraries","description":"Unit tests for all library code in apps/web/src/lib/.

- lib/utils.ts - Utility functions

## Philosophy
- Test real behavior, not mocked interfaces
- Use real data fixtures from corpus
- Detailed assertions with clear failure messages
- Edge cases and error conditions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:09:38.834542336-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:59:40.55333695-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:59:40.55333695-05:00","close_reason":"All P1 child tasks complete. 299 tests passing across lib/: auth (43), parsers (transcript, distillation, delta, artifact-merge), corpusSearch (42), agentMail (42), utils. Only remaining item is P2 GitHub Actions workflow.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-o7a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:03.793407401-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0dk","title":"Feature: Mobile Interactions & Micro-animations","description":"Add the polish and delight that separates 'good' from 'world-class' mobile UX.

## Rationale

The difference between apps that feel 'native' vs 'web' often comes down to:
- Response latency of interactions
- Subtle feedback animations
- Gesture support
- Haptic feedback (where supported)

These micro-interactions communicate quality and care.

## Scope

### Touch Feedback
- Button press states (scale, color shift)
- Card tap feedback
- Navigation item selection feedback
- Verify existing active:scale works well

### Micro-animations
- Page transition animations (already have fade-in, may need refinement)
- List item stagger animations (verify not janky on mobile)
- Skeleton loading states for async content
- Success/error state animations

### Gestures
- Swipe between documents (prev/next)
- Pull-to-refresh on index pages (optional, may not be needed)
- Bottom sheet swipe-to-dismiss (already implemented, verify)

### Performance
- All animations at 60fps
- No jank during scroll
- Immediate response to touch (no delay)

## Anti-Goals

- NOT adding complex gesture navigation (keep it simple)
- NOT implementing haptic feedback (browser support limited)

## Deliverables

- Refined touch feedback across all interactive elements
- Verified/optimized animations for mobile
- Optional gesture navigation between documents
- Performance audit showing 60fps animations

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Touch feedback on all interactive elements
- [ ] Animations run at 60fps (verified in DevTools)
- [ ] No touch delay on any button
- [ ] Page transitions feel smooth","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:26:33.618565684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:26:07.994442637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:26:07.994442637-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile interactions foundation complete: touch-manipulation on all interactive elements for no touch delay, touch feedback via active states, animations verified smooth. Child brenner_bot-0dk.1 (scroll-reveal + copy micro-interactions) is closed. Remaining tasks (60fps verification, swipe nav) are separate beads.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ldh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.791174049-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:25.030571369-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0dk.1","title":"Mobile: scroll-reveal + copy micro-interactions","description":"Implement premium micro-interactions for mobile/reading surfaces:\n- Add AnimatedElement/HeroBackground components for scroll-reveal and hero orbs\n- Add global CSS keyframes + utility classes for reveal/orb/icon animations\n- Integrate CopyButton/ReferenceCopyButton into TranscriptViewer + QuoteBankViewer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:24.88385325-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:11.972503434-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:11.972503434-05:00","close_reason":"Added AnimatedElement/HeroBackground + global reveal/touch utilities; integrated CopyButton/ReferenceCopyButton across transcript/distillation/quotebank viewers","labels":["mobile","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0dk.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:24.912993545-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0fs","title":"Task: Create GitHub Actions workflow for tests","description":"Create CI workflow to run tests on every PR.

## Workflow Configuration
- Trigger on pull_request and push to main
- Run unit tests with Vitest
- Run E2E tests with Playwright
- Report coverage
- Cache dependencies

## Steps
1. Checkout code
2. Setup Bun
3. Install dependencies
4. Run lint
5. Run type-check
6. Run unit tests
7. Run E2E tests
8. Upload coverage report
9. Upload test artifacts

## Requirements
- Fast feedback (target: <5 minutes for unit tests)
- Clear failure messages
- Artifacts preservation for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:15.780691552-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","close_reason":"Created .github/workflows/test.yml with lint, unit tests, build, E2E, typecheck, and CLI test jobs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-djz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.048935463-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.150541808-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.183709782-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-a9d","title":"Component: Jargon tooltip with desktop hover + mobile bottom sheet","description":"# Jargon Component Implementation

## Goal
Create a reusable `<Jargon>` component that displays inline technical terms with:
- Desktop: Hover tooltip via React portal
- Mobile: Tap triggers slide-up bottom sheet
- Accessible: ARIA, keyboard nav, focus management

## Component API

```tsx
interface JargonProps {
  /** Term key to look up in dictionary */
  term: string;
  /** Override display text (defaults to term.term) */
  children?: ReactNode;
  /** Additional styling */
  className?: string;
}

// Usage:
<Jargon term="c-elegans">C. elegans</Jargon>
<Jargon term="level-split" />
```

## Desktop Tooltip Behavior
1. On hover/focus, show floating tooltip above term
2. Tooltip contains: term name, short definition, analogy if present
3. Position: prefer above, flip below if near top of viewport
4. Render via React portal to escape stacking context issues
5. Close on mouse leave with 150ms delay (allows moving to tooltip)

## Mobile Bottom Sheet Behavior
1. On tap, show slide-up bottom sheet with backdrop
2. Sheet contains: full term name, short + long definition, analogy, why, related
3. Lock body scroll while open
4. Close on backdrop tap or X button
5. Smooth spring animation respecting `prefers-reduced-motion`

## Styling (match existing design system)
- Trigger: `decoration-primary/30 decoration-dotted underline underline-offset-[3px]`
- Hover: `hover:decoration-primary/60 hover:text-primary/90`
- Tooltip: `rounded-xl border border-border/50 bg-card/95 backdrop-blur-xl shadow-2xl`
- Sheet: `rounded-t-3xl` with gradient accent line at top
- Use existing CSS animations from globals.css

## Key Implementation Details
- Use `createPortal` for tooltip/sheet to escape overflow:hidden ancestors
- Detect mobile via `window.matchMedia("(max-width: 768px)")`
- Clean up timeouts on unmount
- Handle unknown terms gracefully (render children without styling)

## Files
- `apps/web/src/components/jargon.tsx`
- Depends on: `lib/jargon.ts` (schema bead)

## Testing
- Unit test: render known term, verify tooltip appears
- Unit test: unknown term renders children without styling
- Manual test: hover/tap across breakpoints","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:57:37.260794075-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:05:37.366602282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:05:37.366602282-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Jargon component with desktop hover tooltip (portal-based) and mobile bottom sheet with swipe-to-dismiss. Features: ARIA, keyboard nav, reduced-motion support, glossary deep links. All 545 tests pass.","labels":["component","glossary","mobile"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-a9d","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-64f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:02:54.506232128-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-aeu","title":"TanStack Store: Migrate useLocalStorage Hook to Store","description":"# Goal
Migrate the existing useLocalStorage hook to use TanStack Store for consistency.

## Background
We have a custom useLocalStorage hook:
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/useLocalStorage.ts
export function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T) {
  const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState<T>(() => {
    try {
      const item = window.localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;
    } catch {
      return initialValue;
    }
  });
  // ...
}
```

After implementing TanStack Store for reading positions and compare state, we should:
1. Evaluate if useLocalStorage is still needed
2. Either delete it or have it use Store internally
3. Ensure consistency across all persisted state

## Decision Matrix

| Use Case | Current | After TanStack Store |
|----------|---------|---------------------|
| Reading positions | N/A (none) | readingStore |
| Compare state | N/A (none) | compareStore |
| User preferences | useLocalStorage | preferencesStore |
| Form drafts | N/A | Could use Store |

## Options

### Option A: Delete useLocalStorage
If all use cases are covered by specific stores, delete the generic hook.
Simpler, but less flexible.

### Option B: Keep as Utility
Keep useLocalStorage for one-off values that don't need a store.
E.g., "dismissed welcome banner" flag.

### Option C: Make Store-backed
Reimplement useLocalStorage to use a generic Store internally.
Best of both worlds.

## Recommendation
Option B: Keep useLocalStorage for simple boolean flags, use Store for complex state.

## Migration Steps
1. Audit all uses of useLocalStorage
2. Move complex state to dedicated stores
3. Keep hook for simple flags
4. Document when to use which

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All complex persisted state uses TanStack Store
- [ ] useLocalStorage only used for simple values
- [ ] No duplicate persistence logic
- [ ] Clear documentation on when to use what

## Files to Evaluate
- apps/web/src/hooks/useLocalStorage.ts
- Any component using useLocalStorage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:36.787601565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:32:51.654878107-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:32:51.654878107-05:00","close_reason":"Migration complete: Removed duplicate useReadingPosition from useLocalStorage.ts (TanStack Store version in useReadingPosition.ts is the canonical one). Added documentation explaining when to use useLocalStorage (simple flags) vs TanStack Store (complex state). The readingStore pattern is already established and working.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-aeu","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:44.030717303-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-afui","title":"Create DemoSessionsView component with demo indicator","description":"# Task: Create DemoSessionsView Component

## Context
Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e)
DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc)

## Purpose
A wrapper component that displays demo sessions with a clear 'Demo Mode' indicator banner, followed by session cards that link to demo detail pages.

## CRITICAL: Reuse Existing Components!
Do NOT create a new DemoSessionCard component. The existing `ThreadCard` component already handles `ThreadSummary` data perfectly. We just need to:
1. Convert `DemoThreadSummary` to `ThreadSummary` (they're identical interfaces)
2. Use the existing ThreadCard with demo data
3. Ensure ThreadCard links work for demo sessions (`/sessions/demo-xxx`)

## Component Design

```typescript
// src/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView.tsx

import Link from 'next/link';
import { getDemoThreadSummaries, type DemoThreadSummary } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions';

// Reuse ThreadCard from the sessions page (may need to export it)
// OR duplicate the ThreadCard logic here if it's not exported

interface DemoSessionsViewProps {
  // No props needed - pulls from fixtures
  // Or optionally: sessions?: DemoThreadSummary[]
}

export function DemoSessionsView({ sessions }: DemoSessionsViewProps) {
  const demoSessions = sessions ?? getDemoThreadSummaries();
  
  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      {/* Demo Banner */}
      <div className="rounded-xl border border-amber-200 dark:border-amber-800 bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-950/20 p-5">
        <div className="flex items-start gap-4">
          <div className="flex items-center justify-center size-10 rounded-lg bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900/30 text-amber-600">
            <FlaskConicalIcon className="size-5" />
          </div>
          <div className="space-y-2">
            <h3 className="font-semibold text-amber-900 dark:text-amber-100">
              Demo Mode
            </h3>
            <p className="text-sm text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300">
              These are example sessions demonstrating the BrennerBot research platform.
              Click any session to explore its structure and content.
            </p>
            <div className="flex gap-3 pt-1">
              <Link 
                href="/tutorial/quick-start"
                className="text-sm font-medium text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 hover:underline"
              >
                Try the tutorial →
              </Link>
              <Link 
                href="/corpus"
                className="text-sm font-medium text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 hover:underline"
              >
                Browse the corpus →
              </Link>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Session Cards - reuse ThreadCard pattern */}
      <div className="space-y-3">
        {demoSessions.map((session, index) => (
          <DemoThreadCard 
            key={session.threadId} 
            thread={session} 
            index={index} 
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

// If ThreadCard isn't exported, duplicate the rendering logic here
// The link href will naturally point to /sessions/{demo-xxx} which
// the detail page will handle via demo mode detection
function DemoThreadCard({ thread, index }: { thread: DemoThreadSummary; index: number }) {
  // Same rendering as ThreadCard in sessions/page.tsx
  // Links to /sessions/{thread.threadId}
  // ...
}
```

## Visual Requirements
- Amber/yellow color scheme for demo indicator (distinct from error/warning)
- Flask icon to indicate 'experimental/demo' nature  
- Clear copy explaining what demo mode means
- Links to tutorial and corpus (helpful next steps)
- Session cards look IDENTICAL to real session cards

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Export ThreadCard (Preferred)
1. Export `ThreadCard` from sessions/page.tsx or move to a components file
2. Import and use directly in DemoSessionsView
3. Cleanest approach - no code duplication

### Option B: Duplicate ThreadCard
1. Copy the ThreadCard rendering logic
2. Adapt for demo data
3. More isolated but duplicates code

### Option C: Create shared SessionCard component
1. Extract to `src/components/sessions/SessionCard.tsx`
2. Use in both sessions list page AND DemoSessionsView
3. Best long-term but requires refactoring existing page

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Component renders demo banner prominently
- [ ] Banner is accessible (proper contrast, semantic HTML)
- [ ] Dark mode support (use Tailwind dark: variants)
- [ ] Session cards render below banner
- [ ] Cards link to /sessions/{demo-thread-id}
- [ ] Cards show correct phase badges (compiled, in_critique, etc.)
- [ ] Responsive on mobile
- [ ] Visual parity with real session cards

## Dependencies
- Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc)
- May need to coordinate with demo detail page (brenner_bot-vn19)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:40.208864043-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:21:00.120006451-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-afui","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.83721109-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-afui","title":"Create DemoSessionsView component with demo indicator","description":"# Task: Create DemoSessionsView Component

## Context
Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e)
DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc)

## Purpose
A wrapper component that displays demo sessions with a clear 'Demo Mode' indicator banner, followed by session cards that link to demo detail pages.

## CRITICAL: Reuse Existing Components!
Do NOT create a new DemoSessionCard component. The existing `ThreadCard` component already handles `ThreadSummary` data perfectly. We just need to:
1. Convert `DemoThreadSummary` to `ThreadSummary` (they're identical interfaces)
2. Use the existing ThreadCard with demo data
3. Ensure ThreadCard links work for demo sessions (`/sessions/demo-xxx`)

## Component Design

```typescript
// src/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView.tsx

import Link from 'next/link';
import { getDemoThreadSummaries, type DemoThreadSummary } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions';

// Reuse ThreadCard from the sessions page (may need to export it)
// OR duplicate the ThreadCard logic here if it's not exported

interface DemoSessionsViewProps {
  // No props needed - pulls from fixtures
  // Or optionally: sessions?: DemoThreadSummary[]
}

export function DemoSessionsView({ sessions }: DemoSessionsViewProps) {
  const demoSessions = sessions ?? getDemoThreadSummaries();
  
  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      {/* Demo Banner */}
      <div className="rounded-xl border border-amber-200 dark:border-amber-800 bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-950/20 p-5">
        <div className="flex items-start gap-4">
          <div className="flex items-center justify-center size-10 rounded-lg bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900/30 text-amber-600">
            <FlaskConicalIcon className="size-5" />
          </div>
          <div className="space-y-2">
            <h3 className="font-semibold text-amber-900 dark:text-amber-100">
              Demo Mode
            </h3>
            <p className="text-sm text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300">
              These are example sessions demonstrating the BrennerBot research platform.
              Click any session to explore its structure and content.
            </p>
            <div className="flex gap-3 pt-1">
              <Link 
                href="/tutorial/quick-start"
                className="text-sm font-medium text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 hover:underline"
              >
                Try the tutorial →
              </Link>
              <Link 
                href="/corpus"
                className="text-sm font-medium text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 hover:underline"
              >
                Browse the corpus →
              </Link>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Session Cards - reuse ThreadCard pattern */}
      <div className="space-y-3">
        {demoSessions.map((session, index) => (
          <DemoThreadCard 
            key={session.threadId} 
            thread={session} 
            index={index} 
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

// If ThreadCard isn't exported, duplicate the rendering logic here
// The link href will naturally point to /sessions/{demo-xxx} which
// the detail page will handle via demo mode detection
function DemoThreadCard({ thread, index }: { thread: DemoThreadSummary; index: number }) {
  // Same rendering as ThreadCard in sessions/page.tsx
  // Links to /sessions/{thread.threadId}
  // ...
}
```

## Visual Requirements
- Amber/yellow color scheme for demo indicator (distinct from error/warning)
- Flask icon to indicate 'experimental/demo' nature  
- Clear copy explaining what demo mode means
- Links to tutorial and corpus (helpful next steps)
- Session cards look IDENTICAL to real session cards

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Export ThreadCard (Preferred)
1. Export `ThreadCard` from sessions/page.tsx or move to a components file
2. Import and use directly in DemoSessionsView
3. Cleanest approach - no code duplication

### Option B: Duplicate ThreadCard
1. Copy the ThreadCard rendering logic
2. Adapt for demo data
3. More isolated but duplicates code

### Option C: Create shared SessionCard component
1. Extract to `src/components/sessions/SessionCard.tsx`
2. Use in both sessions list page AND DemoSessionsView
3. Best long-term but requires refactoring existing page

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Component renders demo banner prominently
- [ ] Banner is accessible (proper contrast, semantic HTML)
- [ ] Dark mode support (use Tailwind dark: variants)
- [ ] Session cards render below banner
- [ ] Cards link to /sessions/{demo-thread-id}
- [ ] Cards show correct phase badges (compiled, in_critique, etc.)
- [ ] Responsive on mobile
- [ ] Visual parity with real session cards

 {"id":"brenner_bot-aitt","title":"Manual a11y audit for SessionDashboard shortcuts","description":"# Manual a11y audit for `SessionDashboard` shortcuts

## Why
We added keyboard shortcuts + screen-reader affordances to `SessionDashboard` (shortcuts dialog, phase nav hotkeys, skip link, aria-live).

## What to verify
- Keyboard-only flow:
  - `?` opens/closes shortcuts dialog
  - `←`/`→` phase navigation (when not typing)
  - `1-9` phase jump (when reachable)
  - `Cmd/Ctrl+S` saves (even while typing)
  - `Cmd/Ctrl+E` exports (Shift exports JSON)
- Focus management:
  - Dialog focus trap + restores focus on close
  - Skip link appears on Tab and jumps to main
- Screen reader:
  - `aria-live` announces phase changes
  - `aria-keyshortcuts` is present on key controls
- Visual/accessibility:
  - Focus rings visible on interactive controls
  - Contrast meets WCAG AA (incl. muted text)
  - States are distinguishable without color

## How
- Add/extend a Playwright test using `@axe-core/playwright` for the session dashboard route once it exists.
- Manual smoke check with VoiceOver (macOS) and NVDA (Windows).

 {"id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","title":"TASK: Define Data Migration Strategy for Schema Changes","description":"
## Evolving the Data Model Without Losing User Data

As the Brenner Loop evolves, the data schema will change:
- Adding new fields to HypothesisCard
- Restructuring EvidenceEntry
- New session metadata

Users have existing sessions in LocalStorage. We can't break them.

### Migration Architecture

\`\`\`typescript
// Version marker in all data
interface SessionData {
  _version: number;  // Schema version
  // ... rest of session
}

// Migration registry
const MIGRATIONS: Record<number, Migration> = {
  2: migrateV1ToV2,
  3: migrateV2ToV3,
  // etc.
};

const CURRENT_VERSION = 3;

// Migration function type
type Migration = (data: unknown) => unknown;
\`\`\`

### Migration Flow

\`\`\`
Load session from storage
         │
         ▼
Check _version field
         │
         ├── If current → Use directly
         │
         └── If older → Run migrations
                │
                ▼
         Run each migration in sequence:
         v1 → v2 → v3 → current
                │
                ▼
         Save migrated data back
                │
                ▼
         Continue with session
\`\`\`

### Example Migrations

\`\`\`typescript
// V1 → V2: Add confidence history
function migrateV1ToV2(data: SessionV1): SessionV2 {
  return {
    ...data,
    _version: 2,
    hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({
      ...h,
      confidenceHistory: [{ 
        confidence: h.confidence, 
        timestamp: data.createdAt 
      }]
    }))
  };
}

// V2 → V3: Add prediction lock timestamp
function migrateV2ToV3(data: SessionV2): SessionV3 {
  return {
    ...data,
    _version: 3,
    hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({
      ...h,
      predictions: h.predictions.map(p => ({
        ...p,
        lockedAt: p.locked ? data.updatedAt : null
      }))
    }))
  };
}
\`\`\`

### Migration Safety

1. **Backup before migration**:
\`\`\`typescript
async function safeLoadSession(id: string): Promise<Session> {
  const raw = localStorage.getItem(\`session:\${id}\`);
  const data = JSON.parse(raw);
  
  if (data._version < CURRENT_VERSION) {
    // Backup original
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}:backup:\${data._version}\`, raw);
    
    // Migrate
    const migrated = runMigrations(data);
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}\`, JSON.stringify(migrated));
    
    return migrated;
  }
  
  return data;
}
\`\`\`

2. **Rollback capability**:
\`\`\`typescript
function rollbackMigration(sessionId: string, toVersion: number): boolean {
  const backupKey = \`session:\${sessionId}:backup:\${toVersion}\`;
  const backup = localStorage.getItem(backupKey);
  
  if (backup) {
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${sessionId}\`, backup);
    return true;
  }
  return false;
}
\`\`\`

3. **Version compatibility check**:
\`\`\`typescript
function isVersionSupported(version: number): boolean {
  return version >= MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION && version <= CURRENT_VERSION;
}
\`\`\`

### Testing Migrations

\`\`\`typescript
describe('Migrations', () => {
  test('V1 → V2 adds confidence history', () => {
    const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json');
    const v2Data = migrateV1ToV2(v1Data);
    
    expect(v2Data._version).toBe(2);
    expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory).toBeDefined();
    expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory.length).toBe(1);
  });
  
  test('Full migration path V1 → current', () => {
    const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json');
    const currentData = runMigrations(v1Data);
    
    expect(currentData._version).toBe(CURRENT_VERSION);
    expect(validateSession(currentData)).toBe(true);
  });
});
\`\`\`

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/index.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v1-to-v2.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v2-to-v3.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/__tests__/
\`\`\`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Version field in all persisted data
- [ ] Migration functions for each version bump
- [ ] Backup before migration
- [ ] Rollback capability
- [ ] Migration tests with fixtures
- [ ] Handles corrupted data gracefully

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model)
- 1v26.2 (LocalStorage Persistence)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","title":"Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands

## Objective
CLI commands for scoring sessions, viewing feedback, and comparing scores.

## Implementation Details

### Commands

#### `brenner score <session-id>`
```bash
brenner score RS-20251230

# Output:
Session Score: RS-20251230
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Overall: 72/100 (C)

Dimension Breakdown:
  Paradox Grounding        ████████░░  15/20 (75%)
  Hypothesis Kill Rate     ██████████  20/20 (100%) ★
  Test Discriminability    ██████░░░░  10/15 (67%)
  Assumption Tracking      ████████░░  12/15 (80%)
  Third Alternative        ████░░░░░░   4/10 (40%)
  Experimental Feasibility ██████░░░░   6/10 (60%)
  Adversarial Pressure     ████░░░░░░   5/10 (50%)

Top Signals:
  ✓ At least one hypothesis killed (H-RS20251230-001)
  ✓ Kill linked to test result (T-RS20251230-002)
  ✗ "Both could be wrong" not considered
  ✗ Weak adversarial critique

Run `brenner feedback RS-20251230` for detailed suggestions.
```

#### `brenner score --all`
```bash
brenner score --all

# Output (for all sessions in current program):
Session Scores
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Session           | Score | Grade | Trend
------------------|-------|-------|-------
RS-20251230       |  72   |   C   |  -
RS-20251231       |  78   |   C   |  ↑
RS-20260101       |  85   |   B   |  ↑
RS-20260102       |  81   |   B   |  ↓

Average: 79 (C)
Trend: Improving ↑

Best: RS-20260101 (85, B)
Worst: RS-20251230 (72, C)
```

#### `brenner feedback <session-id>`
```bash
brenner feedback RS-20251230

# Output:
Feedback for RS-20251230
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Summary: Session is progressing but has 3 areas to improve.

[WARNING] Third Alternative Discovery (4/10)
  Issue: "Both could be wrong" not explicitly considered
  
  Suggestion: Explicitly ask: what if ALL current hypotheses are
  wrong? What third alternative would explain the data differently?
  
  Sydney Brenner says: "Both could be wrong." (§103)

[WARNING] Adversarial Pressure (5/10)
  Issue: Insufficient critique of hypotheses
  
  Suggestion: Actively attack your hypotheses. What assumptions
  are you making? What would a skeptic say?

[INFO] Test Discriminability (10/15)
  Issue: Some tests lack mutually exclusive predictions
  
  Suggestion: For each test, ensure hypotheses make DIFFERENT
  predictions. If H1 and H2 predict the same outcome, the test
  cannot distinguish them.
```

#### `brenner leaderboard`
```bash
brenner leaderboard [--dimension=<dim>] [--limit=10]

# Output:
Brenner Method Leaderboard
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Top Sessions by Total Score:
  1. RS-20260101   85 (B)   ████████░░
  2. RS-20260102   81 (B)   ████████░░
  3. RS-20251231   78 (C)   ███████░░░
  4. RS-20251230   72 (C)   ███████░░░

Best by Dimension:
  Paradox Grounding:   RS-20260101 (18/20)
  Hypothesis Kill:     RS-20251230, RS-20260101 (20/20 tie)
  Test Discrimin.:     RS-20260102 (14/15)
```

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qka","title":"Feature: Mobile Reading Experience","description":"Optimize the experience of reading long-form content on mobile devices.

## Rationale

The primary use case for BrennerBot on mobile is READING — users reviewing transcripts, distillations, or quotes. The transcript alone is 40k+ words. This needs to feel effortless.

## User Scenarios

1. Reading through a distillation during a commute (20-45 min)
2. Skimming the transcript to find a remembered quote
3. Comparing sections across documents
4. Reading in bed with dark mode

## Key Optimizations

### Typography
- Ensure text doesn't require zooming (min 16px effective)
- Optimal line length on narrow screens (45-65 characters)
- Proper paragraph spacing for visual breathing room
- Consider hyphenation for justified text (optional)

### Navigation Within Documents
- Sticky section headers that show current position
- Easy jump to section (table of contents)
- Progress indicator (already exists, verify it works well)
- 'Jump to top' accessible without scrolling up

### Visual Comfort
- Proper contrast ratios for outdoor/bright light reading
- Dark mode optimized for OLED screens (already have this)
- No harsh transitions when scrolling

### Interaction
- Smooth scrolling (verify momentum scrolling on iOS)
- Section anchors that work on mobile
- Easy copy/share of specific quotes

## Deliverables

- Typography refinements for mobile
- Document navigation improvements
- Reading-specific UI enhancements

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text readable without zooming on 375px width
- [ ] Can navigate to any section within 2 taps
- [ ] Progress indicator visible during reading
- [ ] Dark mode doesn't cause eye strain
- [ ] Smooth scrolling on iOS and Android","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:26:32.540075774-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile reading experience complete: sticky section indicator, mobile typography optimizations (16px+ clamp), reading-width constraints (65ch), momentum scrolling for iOS, MobileTocFab with BottomSheet for quick navigation. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ldh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.760040476-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:24.994109586-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","title":"Add demo mode logic to sessions page","description":"# Task: Implement Demo Mode in Sessions List Page

## Context
Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e)
DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc)

## Current Behavior (src/app/sessions/page.tsx)
```typescript
let labLockedReason: string | null = null;
if (!labModeEnabled) {
  labLockedReason = 'Lab mode is disabled. Set BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 to enable orchestration.';
}
// ...
{labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}
```

When lab mode is disabled, users see a locked state with no useful content.

## Target Behavior
When lab mode is disabled:
1. Show DemoSessionsView instead of LockedState
2. Demo sessions link to demo detail pages (handled by brenner_bot-vn19)
3. Keep existing behavior when lab mode IS enabled

## Code Changes Required

### 1. Import demo components and data
```typescript
import { DemoSessionsView } from '@/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView';
import { getDemoThreadSummaries } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions';
```

### 2. Detect demo mode
```typescript
const labModeEnabled = isLabModeEnabled();
const isDemoMode = !labModeEnabled;
```

### 3. Update conditional rendering
```typescript
// Instead of:
{labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}

// Use:
{isDemoMode ? (
  <DemoSessionsView />
) : labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}
```

### 4. Adjust header for demo mode
When in demo mode, maybe adjust the header to indicate it:
```typescript
<header className="...">
  <div>
    <h1 className="...">Sessions {isDemoMode && <span className="text-sm text-amber-600">(Demo)</span>}</h1>
    <p className="...">
      {isDemoMode 
        ? 'Example sessions showing the Brenner Loop research workflow'
        : 'Browse and monitor Brenner Loop research sessions'
      }
    </p>
  </div>
  {/* Keep New Session button - links to /sessions/new which has its own demo handling */}
</header>
```

### 5. Consider the 'Local Sessions' section
The page has a 'Local Sessions' section at the bottom that uses `<SessionList />`.
This should probably remain visible in demo mode as it's client-side storage.

## Full Integration Flow
1. User visits /sessions on public site (no lab mode)
2. `isDemoMode = true`
3. Render DemoSessionsView showing demo session cards
4. User clicks a demo session card
5. Navigates to /sessions/demo-xxx
6. Detail page detects demo ID, renders demo content (brenner_bot-vn19)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lab mode enabled: original behavior unchanged (fetches from Agent Mail)
- [ ] Lab mode disabled: shows DemoSessionsView with demo sessions
- [ ] Header indicates demo mode appropriately
- [ ] 'New Session' button still visible and functional
- [ ] 'Local Sessions' section still visible
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] Works on Vercel preview deployment

## Testing
- Local dev: toggle BRENNER_LAB_MODE to verify both paths
  - `BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 bun run dev` → real mode
  - `bun run dev` (no var) → demo mode
- Vercel preview: verify demo mode works (no lab mode in Vercel env)

## Dependencies
- Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc)
- DemoSessionsView component (brenner_bot-afui)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:38.152954927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:23:42.781436239-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.801821203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Current Behavior (src/app/sessions/page.tsx)

```typescript
let labLockedReason: string | null = null;
if (!labModeEnabled) {
  labLockedReason = 'Lab mode is disabled. Set BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 to enable orchestration.';
}
// ...
{labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}
```

When lab mode is disabled, users see a locked state with no useful content.

## Target Behavior
When lab mode is disabled:
1. Show DemoSessionsView instead of LockedState
2. Demo sessions link to demo detail pages (handled by brenner_bot-vn19)
3. Keep existing behavior when lab mode IS enabled

## Code Changes Required

### 1. Import demo components and data
```typescript
import { DemoSessionsView } from '@/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView';
import { getDemoThreadSummaries } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions';
```

### 2. Detect demo mode
```typescript
const labModeEnabled = isLabModeEnabled();
const isDemoMode = !labModeEnabled;
```

### 3. Update conditional rendering
```typescript
// Instead of:
{labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}

// Use:
{isDemoMode ? (
  <DemoSessionsView />
) : labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : ...}
```

### 4. Adjust header for demo mode
When in demo mode, maybe adjust the header to indicate it:
```typescript
<header className="...">
  <div>
    <h1 className="...">Sessions {isDemoMode && <span className="text-sm text-amber-600">(Demo)</span>}</h1>
    <p className="...">
      {isDemoMode 
        ? 'Example sessions showing the Brenner Loop research workflow'
        : 'Browse and monitor Brenner Loop research sessions'
      }
    </p>
  </div>
  {/* Keep New Session button - links to /sessions/new which has its own demo handling */}
</header>
```

### 5. Consider the 'Local Sessions' section
The page has a 'Local Sessions' section at the bottom that uses `<SessionList />`.
This should probably remain visible in demo mode as it's client-side storage.

## Full Integration Flow
1. User visits /sessions on public site (no lab mode)
2. `isDemoMode = true`
3. Render DemoSessionsView showing demo session cards
4. User clicks a demo session card
5. Navigates to /sessions/demo-xxx
6. Detail page detects demo ID, renders demo content (brenner_bot-vn19)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lab mode enabled: original behavior unchanged (fetches from Agent Mail)
- [ ] Lab mode disabled: shows DemoSessionsView with demo sessions
- [ ] Header indicates demo mode appropriately
- [ ] 'New Session' button still visible and functional
- [ ] 'Local Sessions' section still visible
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] Works on Vercel preview deployment

## Testing
- Local dev: toggle BRENNER_LAB_MODE to verify both paths
  - `BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 bun run dev` → real mode
  - `bun run dev` (no var) → demo mode
- Vercel preview: verify demo mode works (no lab mode in Vercel env)

## Dependencies
- Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc)
- DemoSessionsView component (brenner_bot-afui)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:38.152954927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:16.565386612-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:16.565386612-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.801821203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-qtsb","title":"EPIC: The Complete Brenner Loop System","description":"# The Complete Brenner Loop System

## Vision Statement
Transform BrennerBot from a corpus browser into a full-featured **research methodology lab** that operationalizes the Brenner approach to discriminative hypothesis testing.

## Background & Rationale
The existing BrennerBot provides access to Brenner's primary source material (transcripts, distillations, quote banks) and explains the operators. But the real value of Brenner's methodology lies in **active application**:

1. **Formulating testable hypotheses** with falsifiable predictions
2. **Applying operators systematically** (Level Split, Exclusion Test, Object Transpose, Scale Check)
3. **Running discriminative tests** that could actually rule out possibilities
4. **Updating beliefs based on evidence** in a structured way
5. **Synthesizing insights** through multi-perspective analysis

This epic represents the complete transformation from 'documentation about methodology' to 'interactive methodology lab.'

## Core Insight
The Brenner approach is fundamentally about **preferring to be ruled out rather than ruled in**. A good hypothesis is one that makes bold predictions that could be wrong. The system should guide users toward thinking that way, not just explain the concepts.

## Success Criteria
- Users can run complete research sessions from hypothesis to conclusion
- The system guides users toward better (more discriminative) thinking
- Multi-agent synthesis provides genuine adversarial pressure
- All work products are durable artifacts with clear audit trails
- The experience is addictive because it genuinely improves thinking

## Key Dependencies
- Existing corpus infrastructure (complete)
- Tutorial system (in progress)
- Agent Mail integration (foundation exists)
- GA4 analytics (complete)

## Technical Constraints
- No vendor AI APIs in web app - coordinate via Agent Mail + external agents
- Must work gracefully when agents unavailable (degraded but functional)
- Must support offline-first for reading/reviewing (PWA future)

## Sub-Components (Each an Epic)
1. Hypothesis Engine - Core data models, cards, evolution tracking
2. Operator Application System - Interactive guided sessions for each operator
3. Multi-Agent Synthesis - Devil's Advocate, Experiment Designer, Brenner Channeler
4. Evidence Ledger - Test tracking, result recording, belief updates
5. Artifact Generation - Research Briefs, exports, structured outputs
6. Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector search, confound detection
7. Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, state machines, progress
8. Infrastructure & API - Persistence, real-time, collaboration foundation

## Out of Scope for v1
- Multi-user real-time collaboration (deferred to v2)
- Public API (deferred to v2)
- Mobile native apps (web PWA sufficient for v1)

6. Semantic Search (ukd1) - Embeddings, similarity search, quote matching
7. Session Experience (reew) - Context, state machine, phase transitions, coach
8. Infrastructure (1v26) - Comprehensive storage layer, persistence, analytics

Success criteria met:
- Users can run complete research sessions ✓
- System guides toward discriminative thinking ✓
- Multi-agent synthesis provides adversarial pressure ✓
- Durable artifacts with audit trails ✓
- 70+ TypeScript files implementing the vision ✓

The Complete Brenner Loop System is operational.","labels":["brenner-loop","vision"]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qzr","title":"Web orchestration: Windows absolute projectKey should trigger ensure_project","description":"## Problem
`apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts` currently decides whether to call Agent Mail `ensure_project` using a POSIX-only check:

- `projectKey.startsWith("/")`

This skips `ensure_project` for Windows absolute paths (e.g. `C:\\repo` or `\\\\server\\share`), causing downstream Agent Mail calls to fail or behave inconsistently.

## Root cause
We’re conflating “absolute path” with “starts with /”.

## Fix
Use `isAbsolute(projectKey)` from `node:path` (cross-platform) for the decision.

## Acceptance
- Windows absolute project keys call `ensure_project`.
- Existing POSIX behavior unchanged.
- Web tests still pass (`cd apps/web && bun run test`).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.022060056-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","close_reason":"Use node:path isAbsolute(projectKey) for ensure_project gating (Windows-safe); apps/web tests pass","labels":["agent-mail","orchestration","web","windows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qzr","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.048932296-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
